prompt:
The dev server's hot-reload loop sits on the dirwatch package (snapshot a dir, diff, rebuild). Works, but three long-standing complaints from the frontend folks finally got a sprint slot:

1) Ignore patterns. Editors litter the tree with *.tmp and swap files and every save triggers a rebuild storm. Need TakeIgnoring(dir, patterns) — same snapshot as Take, but a file is skipped when any pattern matches either its base name or its full slash-relative path, with path.Match semantics (so * doesn't cross a slash: build/* hides build/out.js but not build/sub/out.js — that asymmetry is intended, don't get clever). A malformed pattern should come back as an error, not be silently dropped. Nil patterns must behave exactly like Take.

2) Rename detection. When someone renames logo-old.svg to logo.svg we currently do a delete-rebuild + add-rebuild. New DiffRenames(old, cur): when exactly one removed path and exactly one added path share a content hash, report it as a rename (new Rename struct with From/To, Changes gets a Renamed field, sorted by From) and keep it out of Added/Removed. If the hash match is ambiguous — several candidates on either side — don't guess, leave them as plain adds/removes. Plain Diff must keep reporting renames as add+remove like today; existing callers depend on it.

3) Debouncing. Big saves land as several writes over ~100ms. Give me a Debouncer: NewDebouncer(quiet, now) where now is an injected clock func (we test with a fake clock, and so should you — no sleeps). Mark(path) records or refreshes activity; Ready() returns, sorted, every path whose last Mark is at least the quiet period old, removes them from tracking, and a later Mark starts a fresh cycle.

watcher_test.go is the acceptance contract. dirwatch_test.go pins current behavior and every test in it keeps passing. Both files are read-only for you. Done = go test -race -timeout 30s ./... passing.
